name: Edge Identity Mandate
description: Edge-identity → budget → caps as a verified mandate cell: an SSH public key (or any access-edge identity) bound to a dregg account, a spend ceiling, and an attenuated tool-set (granted ⊑ held — no amplification), with enrol / spend / revoke as witnessed turns. A thin deploy-side adapter lowers a mandate cell to an OpenSSH authorized_keys forced-command line — a pure function of the committed cell state.

heap_collections.REC_COLL: 0x0000ED6E — the deploy-facing enrolment record (account / ssh-pubkey / canonical caps / brain), chunked strings folded into the state commitment; the authorized_keys adapter reads these back

axes.core: the FactoryDescriptor (mandate_factory_descriptor) + the mandate_cell_program (the identity/budget/caps invariants the executor re-enforces on every touching turn)
axes.service: the invoke() front door (src/service.rs) — a typed InterfaceDescriptor over enrol / spend / revoke / view; enrol/spend/revoke are Replayable + Signature-gated, view is a Serviced OFE read seam
axes.card: the deos-view card (src/card.rs) — the mandate dashboard as a renderer-independent deos.ui.* view-tree (a live spend gauge toward the sealed budget + the three mutating-method buttons)
axes.adapter: the deploy-side authorized_keys adapter (authorized_keys_line / authorized_keys) — a PURE FUNCTION from a mandate cell to an OpenSSH forced-command line; a revoked mandate emits no line

composes.dregg-agent: session::parse_caps_confined validates an enrolled caps string against the real tool grammar under Confinement::Hosted (a raw shell is refused at enrol)
composes.dregg-auth: a dregg_auth::Grant lifts (held_from_grant) into the operator's HELD mandate, so 'enrol mints a mandate no wider than the grant' is literal
composes.agent-orchestration: the SAME Mandate attenuation lattice (granted ⊑ held, sub-budget, scoped tool-set) it proves, re-pointed at an access-edge subject and the caps-token vocabulary

seam: PARTIAL — the budget bound (AffineLe(spent ≤ budget)), the WriteOnce identity/economics, the monotone meter/revocation, and the no-replay epoch are REAL verified turns the executor re-enforces on enrol / spend / revoke. The enrolment-record heap strings are an executor-side ledger mirror (there is no heap-write effect), and standing up the live SSH edge (a real sshd whose AuthorizedKeysCommand serves the adapter's output) is the reviewed deploy step, not this crate.
